bugs.error_message: version solving failed: flutter_matter basic_utils ^5.8.2 requires pointycastle ^4.0.0, but example depends on pointycastle ^3.1.3
bugs.root_cause: basic_utils was bumped to ^5.8.2, whose floor (5.8.2) depends on pointycastle 4.x; example app pins pointycastle ^3.1.3
bugs.fix: lowered plugin constraint to basic_utils ^5.7.0 so pub can resolve a 5.7.x build using pointycastle 3.x (resolved to pointycastle 3.9.1)

bugs.root_cause: Property access on potentially null/undefined value
bugs.fix: Added null safety (optional chaining or null check)

bugs.error_message: PlatformException(DeviceControlCallException, Create Controller failed, null, null) - commissioning fails with code -2 at 'Controller setup' step
bugs.root_cause: newDeviceControllerCall() calls [ZGMTRControllerFactory sharedInstance] startControllerOnExistingFabric:/startControllerOnNewFabric: but never checks that the factory was actually started. Startup happens once at plugin registration via InitializeZGMTR() (FlutterMatterPlugin.m), using the deprecated startup: API which only returns a bare BOOL with no error detail; if it silently fails (e.g. keychain access rejected after switching Xcode signing teams) both fabric-start calls return nil and the exception message gives no clue why.
bugs.fix: Added NSLog diagnostics in InitializeZGMTR() (factory startup failure, FabricKeys nil, both fabric-start calls nil) and a factory.isRunning check + isRunning value in the thrown exception reason in newDeviceControllerCall(), so the next run's native console reveals the real cause instead of a generic 'Create Controller failed'.

bugs.error_message: pairDevice error Instance of 'CallPlatformException' - no detail, commissioning fails silently at Secure session step
bugs.root_cause: CallPlatformException held a 'message' field (built as '[$code] Call $methodName params ... failed' in controller.dart _requestPlatform) but never overrode toString(), so any '$e' string interpolation (including CommissioningController._addLog('pairDevice error $e $s')) printed the default 'Instance of CallPlatformException' instead of the actual message/error code.
bugs.fix: Made CallPlatformException implement Exception and added toString() returning the message, so the real platform error code/method/params now show up in logs.
